name: Convoy Escort
description: Escort CONVOY NX-42 across the Atlantic. Two Akula-class submarines are repositioning to intercept.
briefing:
CLASSIFICATION: SECRET

TO: Commander, Escort Group NOVEMBER
FROM: COMNAVSURFLANT
DTG: 081600Z NOV 1985

CONVOY NX-42 is underway from Norfolk, bound for the UK. Two vessels: MV Atlantic Conveyor and MV Nordic Ferry. Military cargo. Arrival is non-negotiable.

SIGINT intercepted repositioning orders for at least two Akula-class boats twenty hours ago. Bearing tracks and traffic analysis put them somewhere across the convoy's projected track. They know the route. They probably have it to within twenty miles.

The convoy cannot stop. The convoy cannot deviate. They are on a fixed course at 14 knots and they are not your asset to maneuver.

Your task force: USS Hayler (DDG), USS McClusky (FFG). DAGGER 03 P-3C from Lajes is assigned to your escort group for wide-area search. Use it.

The P-3 is the only asset you have that can cover the search box the convoy is transiting in the next four hours. Get it out ahead and find those boats before they find the merchant ships.

Rules of engagement: weapons free on confirmed hostile subsurface contacts.
